accepted.reason: Candidate filtering over sampled points is a practical iterative search kernel with branching and threshold updates.
Reverse-complement mapping is a compact sequence-processing kernel with straightforward loop optimization potential.
Bitwise Hilbert curve transforms are deterministic low-level kernels useful for algorithmic optimization traces.

skipped.reason: Compression wrapper around external LZO library memory APIs, not a standalone Python kernel target.
Mixed utility module with IO/UI helpers and missing imports; low cohesion for focused optimization training.
Large binding layer around external nmsg runtime and sockets, not suitable for standalone conversion examples.
Application-specific chess/MPI engine integration with heavy external state and dependencies.
Complex scientific integration code tied to scipy callbacks and project-specific modules; too framework-bound.
Memory-allocation tutorial code emphasizing pointer ownership semantics rather than reusable optimization kernel tasks.
FFI wrapper for Harfbuzz/Freetype shaping APIs, dominated by bindings and external object lifecycle logic.
